# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.3.0

This release makes app-server recovery ownership explicit and keeps recovery active after a
temporary Codex disconnect.

## Highlights

- Installs the matching immutable Bridge release that provides the app-server watchdog command.
- Persists the final installer-selected app-server ownership mode on reinstalls.
- Coordinates bridge and watchdog recovery through a non-blocking shared lock without consuming
  recovery budget during contention.
- Bounds app-server daemon control commands and cleans up timed-out or cancelled children.
- Preserves SessionSpace identity and generation when publishing review completion callbacks.

## Upgrade notes

- Existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state, session spaces, and installer-owned units
  are preserved by the v0.3.0 installer.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
